name: Settings Belong to the Shell
designPattern: Frame-Owned Settings Drawer / Shell-Level Preference Surface
requirements: Tests ARE the Spec roadmap principle

userStory.asA: user of a framed Bijou app
userStory.iWant: a standard shell-owned settings drawer I can open without leaving my current page
userStory.soThat: I can adjust global preferences in place and trust that the shell owns the interaction coherently

acceptanceCriteria: createFramedApp() can expose a standard settings drawer without each page implementing its own overlay plumbing
Settings are declared as structured sections and rows rather than ad hoc markdown or custom drawer content
The settings drawer is anchored to the left edge and keeps the current page visible behind it
When settings are open, the drawer owns local keyboard and mouse interaction instead of the underlying page
The settings list can scroll independently when content exceeds the drawer height
Mouse click and wheel interactions inside the drawer do not leak through to the underlying page
The shell can expose a standard command-palette entry and binding to open settings
The help surface can reflect settings-layer controls while the drawer is open
The DOGFOOD docs app can adopt the standard settings drawer as a proving surface with minimal custom code
backwardCompatibility: Existing framed apps remain valid without providing settings. The settings drawer is additive in v0 and does not require page layout changes or custom overlay rewrites.

inScope: Mouse click and wheel support inside the drawer
DOGFOOD docs app as the first proving surface
outScope: Editable text fields or arbitrary embedded controls in settings rows
A generalized settings framework for every app outside the frame shell
Implementing the full Focus Owns Input stack in the same slice

testPlan.goldenPath: Write failing app-frame tests that prove the shell can open a standard settings drawer, that the drawer consumes keyboard and mouse input while open, that long settings lists scroll independently, and that the command palette can open settings. Then add a docs-preview regression that toggles one visible DOGFOOD preference through the drawer.
edgeCases: Compact terminal width with a narrow drawer
Settings list longer than the viewport height
Opening help or command palette while settings are already open
Mouse wheel scrolling over the drawer vs outside it
A framed app that does not provide any settings content
testPlan.fuzzStress: Not a priority in v0. The core risk is routing precedence and shell coherence, not high-throughput preference mutation.
failureCases: The settings drawer opens but arrow keys still mutate the underlying page
Mouse interactions inside settings click through to page panes or overlays below
The drawer cannot scroll a long preference list in compact terminals
Settings are implemented as one app-specific drawer instead of a reusable shell feature
The shell help and palette become inconsistent with the settings layer

estimatedComplexity.linesOfCode: ~250-450 lines across app-frame state, settings rendering, input routing, palette integration, and docs-shell adoption, plus targeted regressions
estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(S) per settings interaction where S is the number of visible or declared settings rows; v0 should keep the active interaction model bounded and simple
